title: Abrahamia pauciflora
taxonomy: Abrahamia pauciflora belongs to the family Anacardiaceae, a family of flowering plants that also includes cashews and poison ivy. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Abrahamia. It is native to the island of Madagascar, which is known for its unique biodiversity. Abrahamia pauciflora is classified as a deciduous shrub, with small, fragrant flowers and edible fruits. The plant's classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties.
